websites.title: Streamflow drought assessment and forecasting tool
websites.alt: A map of Florida showing the streamflow drought status at streamgage locations. One gage is selected, and a window in the bottom left shows a timeseries of observed and forecast streamflow conditions at that site

websites.title: Modeling streamflow drought
websites.alt: An abstract compilation of snippets from charts that illustrate how streamflow drought is forecast

websites.title: Water availability in the United States
websites.alt: Map showing water limitation in the lower 48 United States by watershed. The background visualization shows monthly dynamics in water limitation from 2010 to 2020.

websites.title: Earth is in Flux
websites.alt: Six tiles arranged in a grid of 3 columns and 2 rows show thumbnails for visualizations in the Earth in Flux chart gallery

websites.title: Managing redband trout for climate resilience
websites.alt: Illustrated river with two cartoon redband trout swimming in it in front of a data visualization that shows how cold- and warm-adapted redband trout are affected by water temperature. The visualization demonstrates that cold-adapted redband trout suffer from thermal stress at lower overall temperatures than warm-adapted redband trout. Text on the visualization includes labels for each line: "cold-adapted redband trout" and "warm-adapted redband trout" and a label for "thermal stress."

websites.title: Unequal access to water
websites.alt: Two contrasting images of water sources with the top image showing a close-up of a hand holding a clear glass being filled with water from a kitchen faucet. The bottom image shows a rustic landscape with a blue well tank labeled WELL-X-TROL with a wired fence and dry grass in the background.

websites.title: Water bottling across the U.S.
websites.alt: A map of Texas showing the count of bottled water facilities in each county.

websites.title: Five droughts that changed history
websites.alt: An image of dried, cracked mud overlain by a chart with long vertical bands representing drought events

websites.title: What is streamflow drought?
websites.alt: An illustration of a partially dried-up streambed, wilted sunflower, and brown landscape depicting streamflow drought, which follows after meteorological and agricultural drought.

websites.title: The Water Cycle
websites.description: Zoomable version of the water cycle diagram
websites.alt: A zoomed out view of a water cycle diagram

websites.title: Pools and Fluxes in the Water Cycle
websites.description: All of the water on Earth
websites.alt: An illustration of the water cycle showing precipitation and runoff.

websites.title: How we Monitor Stream Temperature in the Delaware River Basin
websites.alt: A map of stream reaches in the Delaware River Basin artistically embedded in a line chart showing stream temperature throughtout the year.

websites.title: From Snow to Flow
websites.description: What changing snowmelt means for water in the Western U.S.
websites.alt: An image of mountains near a lake covered in snow, with peak-like line charts artistically placed in the background.

websites.title: How wildfires threaten U.S. water supplies
websites.description: Communities across the United States and the globe rely on clean water flowing from forested watersheds. But these water source areas are impacted by the effects of wildfire.
websites.alt: A thumbnail showing a burn scar from a wildfire, with a line chart of the total acres burned by wildfires abstractly in the background.

websites.title: Gages Through the Ages
websites.description: How the history of streamgaging reflects the evolving water needs of the nation
websites.alt: A cartogram map of the U.S. made from area charts depicting the total number of USGS streamgages in each state over time.

websites.title: Water use in the U.S., 2015
websites.description: We all depend on water every day, ranging from the water from our faucets, to the food we eat, to much of the electricity we use.
websites.alt: A bubble map with bubbles for each U.S. county, showing total water use in 2015.

websites.title: U.S. Water Use from 1950-2015
websites.description: How much water do we use?
websites.alt: A map of the U.S. with each state area scaled to total water use in 2015

websites.title: Shifts in Fish Habitat Under Climate Change
websites.description: Lakes in Wisconsin are getting warmer, and fish communities are changing as a result.
websites.alt: An image of two fish silhouettes with a line chart overlaid artistically.

websites.title: Microplastics in the Great Lakes
websites.description: Where in our waterways are microplastics found?
websites.alt: A sankey chart showing how much of the water stored in Lake Mead is distributed among California, Nevada, Arizona, and Mexico.

websites.title: OWDI Lower Colorado Drought
websites.alt: A sankey chart showing how much of the water stored in Lake Mead is distributed among California, Nevada, Arizona, and Mexico.
